endpoints.description: Simple liveness check; always returns {status:'ok'} when the process is up.
endpoints.menubar_value: Primary 'is the process alive?' ping — cheapest possible check, no DB hit.

endpoints.description: Kubernetes-style liveness probe; same as /health but returns HealthCheckResponse envelope.
endpoints.menubar_value: Alternate liveness check used by watchdog probes.

endpoints.description: Readiness probe: checks DB connectivity and bridge ring membership. Returns 503 when draining or not in ring.
endpoints.menubar_value: Deep health including DB + ring — show yellow badge when 503 vs green for 200.

endpoints.description: Startup probe; returns 503 until the app fully initialises (schedulers started, bridge registered).
List all accounts with full per-account quota, usage, auth, and rate-limit state.

endpoints.menubar_value: Core data source for the account list menu — status, remaining credits, reset times, rate-limit state per account.

endpoints.description: Aggregate quota windows (primary/secondary/monthly) plus 7-day cost and error-rate metrics across all accounts.
endpoints.menubar_value: Best single summary for the menu bar popover: pool-wide remaining credits, next reset time, 7-day cost, error rate.

endpoints.description: Per-account average remaining credits over the last N hours (1-168). Query param: hours (default 24).
endpoints.menubar_value: Trend sparkline data: plot each account's credit burn over the chosen window.

endpoints.description: Per-account snapshot for a specific quota window. Query param: window ('primary'|'secondary').

Full dashboard payload: all AccountSummary objects, quota windows, MetricsTrends (requests/tokens/cost/error_rate series), depletion forecasts, weekly credit pace. Query param: timeframe ('1d'|'7d'|'30d', default '7d').

endpoints.description: Paginated recent proxy request log. Supports filters: limit (1-1000), offset, search, accountId[], apiKeyId[], status[], model[], since, until.

endpoints.menubar_value: Recent activity feed — show last 5-10 requests with model, status, cost, latency in the popover.

endpoints.description: Returns distinct filter facets (accountIds[], modelOptions[], apiKeys[], statuses[]) usable as filter chip data for the log view.
Current and latest agent-lb version, whether an update is available, and a link to releases.
endpoints.menubar_value: Show update-available badge on the menu bar icon when updateAvailable=true.

endpoints.description: Current quota planner configuration: mode, schedule, prewarm settings, forecast params.
Recent quota planner decisions (warmup actions or no-ops). Query param: limit (1-200, default 50).
endpoints.menubar_value: Show last warmup decision and its status (skipped/executed/failed) in a detail section.

endpoints.description: Pause a specific account, removing it from active load-balancer rotation.
endpoints.menubar_value: Quick-action toggle from the account context menu — pause a rate-limited or misbehaving account.

endpoints.description: Reactivate a paused or deactivated account back into load-balancer rotation.
endpoints.menubar_value: Companion to pause — re-enable an account from the context menu.

auth_notes: When AGENT_LB_DASHBOARD_AUTH_MODE=disabled (the local setup), all /api/* dashboard endpoints accept requests with no credentials — no token, cookie, or header required. The auth middleware short-circuits to an anonymous DashboardRequestAuth with mode='disabled'. In standard mode the dashboard requires a session cookie obtained via POST /api/auth/login (TOTP optional). In trusted_header mode a configured HTTP header from a trusted-proxy CIDR carries the actor identity. Since auth is disabled locally, the menu bar app can call all /api/* endpoints directly from localhost with no auth headers. The three /health/* endpoints are always unauthenticated.
service_control:
Check running status: `launchctl list com.aneyman.agent-lb` — PID column is non-empty when running, exit code 0. The process liveness is also confirmed by GET http://127.0.0.1:2455/health returning {status:'ok'}.

Start (if stopped): `launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.aneyman.agent-lb.plist`
Stop: `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.aneyman.agent-lb.plist`
Restart (kill + relaunch via watchdog): `launchctl kickstart -k gui/501/com.aneyman.agent-lb`
Full restart via unload/load: `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.aneyman.agent-lb.plist && launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.aneyman.agent-lb.plist`

Watchdog label: com.aneyman.agent-lb-watchdog (separate plist, keeps the main service alive — do not unload unless you want the service permanently stopped).

Detect running vs stopped:
1. Fast process check: `launchctl list com.aneyman.agent-lb | awk 'NR==2{print $1}'` — prints a PID when running, '-' when stopped.
2. HTTP check: GET /health/ready returns HTTP 200 + {status:'ok'} when fully up, HTTP 503 when starting or draining, connection refused when down.
3. HTTP check: GET /health/startup returns HTTP 200 when startup is complete, 503 while initialising.
